# OpenWeatherMap Connector - Full Documentation

# OpenWeatherMap Connector 1.0

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for OpenWeatherMap (OpenWeatherMap Connector) enables Mule applications to access
the OpenWeatherMap API.

For information about compatibility and fixed issues, refer to the OpenWeatherMap Connector Release
Notes.

## Before You Begin

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- The OpenWeatherMap Connectorâs API
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)
- Elements and global elements in a Mule flow
- How to create a Mule app using Anypoint Code Builder or Anypoint Studio

Before creating an app, you must have:

- Credentials to access the OpenWeatherMap target resource
- Anypoint Platform
- The latest versions of Anypoint Code Builder or Anypoint Studio

## Next Step

After you complete the prerequisites, youâre ready to create an app and configure the connector
using [Anypoint Studio](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio) or
[Anypoint Code Builder](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-acb).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Use Exchange to Discover Connectors, Templates, and Examples](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [OpenWeatherMap Connector Reference](openweathermap-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# OpenWeatherMap Connector 1.0 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## Configurations

---

### Config

Default configuration.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Config_ApiKey">Api Key</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>The connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID used to reference this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### API Key

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>apiKey</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key used to authenticate requests to the OpenWeatherMap API.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>baseUri</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Base URI for the OpenWeatherMap API endpoint.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Supported Operations

- [Get Current Weather](#GetCurrentWeather)
- [Get Timestamp Data](#GetTimestampData)
- [Get Weather Overview](#GetWeatherOverview)

## Operations

## Get Current Weather

`<openweathermap:get-current-weather>`

Get Current Weather.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>lat</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Latitude coordinate of the location.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>lon</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Longitude coordinate of the location.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>units</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>standard</p></li><li><p>metric</p></li><li><p>imperial</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Temperature units for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>lang</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Language code for the response text.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- OPENWEATHERMAP:BAD_REQUEST
- OPENWEATHERMAP:CLIENT_ERROR
- OPENWEATHERMAP:CONNECTIVITY
- OPENWEATHERMAP:FORBIDDEN
- OPENWEATHERMAP:NOT_FOUND
- OPENWEATHERMAP:SECURITY
- OPENWEATHERMAP:SERVER_ERROR
- OPENWEATHERMAP:TIMEOUT
- OPENWEATHERMAP:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- OPENWEATHERMAP:UNAUTHORIZED

## Get Timestamp Data

`<openweathermap:get-timestamp-data>`

Get Timestamp Data.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>lat</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Latitude coordinate of the location.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>lon</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Longitude coordinate of the location.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Unix Timestamp</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timestamp (Unix time, UTC time zone). Data is available from January 1st, 1979 till 4 days ahead.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>units</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>standard</p></li><li><p>metric</p></li><li><p>imperial</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Temperature units for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>lang</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Language code for the response text.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- OPENWEATHERMAP:BAD_REQUEST
- OPENWEATHERMAP:CLIENT_ERROR
- OPENWEATHERMAP:CONNECTIVITY
- OPENWEATHERMAP:FORBIDDEN
- OPENWEATHERMAP:NOT_FOUND
- OPENWEATHERMAP:SECURITY
- OPENWEATHERMAP:SERVER_ERROR
- OPENWEATHERMAP:TIMEOUT
- OPENWEATHERMAP:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- OPENWEATHERMAP:UNAUTHORIZED

## Get Weather Overview

`<openweathermap:get-weather-overview>`

Get Weather Overview.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>lat</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Latitude coordinate of the location.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>lon</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Longitude coordinate of the location.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>units</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>standard</p></li><li><p>metric</p></li><li><p>imperial</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Temperature units for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>lang</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Language code for the response text.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- OPENWEATHERMAP:BAD_REQUEST
- OPENWEATHERMAP:CLIENT_ERROR
- OPENWEATHERMAP:CONNECTIVITY
- OPENWEATHERMAP:FORBIDDEN
- OPENWEATHERMAP:NOT_FOUND
- OPENWEATHERMAP:SECURITY
- OPENWEATHERMAP:SERVER_ERROR
- OPENWEATHERMAP:TIMEOUT
- OPENWEATHERMAP:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- OPENWEATHERMAP:UNAUTHORIZED

## Types

### TLS

Configures TLS to provide secure communications for the Mule app.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Protocols</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Cipher Suites</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trust Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Truststore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Keystore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Revocation Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#StandardRevocationCheck">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CustomOcspResponder">Custom OCSP Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CrlFile">CRL File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a revocation checking mechanism.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Truststore

Configures the truststore for TLS.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the truststore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the truststore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Insecure</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule stops performing certificate validations. Setting this to <code>true</code> can make connections vulnerable to attacks.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Keystore

Configures the keystore for the TLS protocol. The keystore you generate contains a private key and a
public certificate.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the keystore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the key to use when the keystore contains multiple private keys. By default, Mule uses the first key in the file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the private key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the keystore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

Configures standard revocation checks for TLS certificates.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Only End Entities</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Which elements to verify in the certificate chain:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Verify only the last element in the certificate chain.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>false</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Verify all elements in the certificate chain.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefer Crls</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Check the Certification Revocation List (CRL) for certificate validity.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>false</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Use the Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP) to check certificate validity.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>No Fallback</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use the secondary method to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Use the method that wasnât specified in the <strong>Prefer Crls</strong> field (the secondary method) to check certificate validity.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>false</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Do not use the secondary method to check certificate validity.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Soft Fail</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if the revocation server canât be reached or is busy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Avoid verification failure.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>false</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Allow the verification to fail.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom OCSP Responder

Configures a custom OCSP responder for certification revocation checks.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the OCSP responder.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cert Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response. If specified, the alias must be in the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### CRL File

Specifies the location of the certification revocation list (CRL) file.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the CRL file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

Configures a reconnection strategy for an operation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

Configures a standard reconnection strategy, which specifies how often to reconnect and how many
reconnection attempts the connector source or operation can make.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How many reconnection attempts the Mule app can make.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

Configures a forever reconnection strategy by which the connector source or operation attempts to
reconnect at a specified frequency for as long as the Mule app runs.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

Configures an expiration policy strategy.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the maximum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Max Idle Time</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Error Mapping

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Source</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ANY</p></li><li><p>REDELIVERY_EXHAUSTED</p></li><li><p>TRANSFORMATION</p></li><li><p>EXPRESSION</p></li><li><p>SECURITY</p></li><li><p>CLIENT_SECURITY</p></li><li><p>SERVER_SECURITY</p></li><li><p>ROUTING</p></li><li><p>CONNECTIVITY</p></li><li><p>RETRY_EXHAUSTED</p></li><li><p>TIMEOUT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Error source type to map.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Target error type to map to.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>
